title: helianthemum vesicarium var. ciliatum
intro: Helianthemum vesicarium var. ciliatum, also known as rockrose or sticky rockrose, is a type of flowering plant in the Cistaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Helianthemum vesicarium var. ciliatum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
